savedState.boardInfo: Proof of Concept - Not Endorsed. Evidence-informed strategy and delivery model prepared from public material available on 20 July 2026. Illustrative projects and mappings require official, fiscal, legal, operational and devolved-government review before use.

pageInfo.p1: Burnham proposes a ten-year living-standards plan and public control of essential systems. Evidence: https://www.andyburnham.org.uk/speech-29-june and https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/warm-homes-plan/warm-homes-plan-html
pageInfo.p2: The page reflects Burnham’s “good growth in every postcode” proposition and the current industrial-strategy record. Evidence: https://www.andyburnham.org.uk/speech-29-june and https://www.gov.uk/government/collections/the-uks-modern-industrial-strategy-2025
pageInfo.p3: The housing pathway combines Burnham’s council housebuilding and Housing First proposals with the 2026–2036 Social and Affordable Homes Programme. Evidence: https://www.andyburnham.org.uk/speech-29-june and https://www.gov.uk/guidance/social-and-affordable-homes-programme-sahp-2026-to-2036
pageInfo.p4: The logic combines Burnham’s call for academic–technical parity and hope for young people with the current Youth Guarantee. Evidence: https://www.andyburnham.org.uk/speech-29-june and https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/get-britain-working-an-update-on-the-jobs-and-careers-service/get-britain-working-an-update-on-the-jobs-and-careers-service
pageInfo.p5: The prevention and integration pathway reflects Burnham’s health background and the three shifts in the 10 Year Health Plan. Evidence: https://labour.org.uk/updates/members-updates/andy-burnham-elected-leader-of-the-labour-party/ and https://www.gov.uk/government/collections/10-year-health-plan-for-england
pageInfo.p6: This page aligns community safety, lawful border administration and justice-system performance. Evidence: https://www.gov.uk/missions/safer-streets and https://www.gov.uk/government/speeches/immigration-and-asylum-bill-second-reading-opening-speech
pageInfo.p7: The pathway joins Burnham’s public-control theme to current warm-homes, clean-power and water-reform evidence. Evidence: https://www.andyburnham.org.uk/speech-29-june, https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/warm-homes-plan/warm-homes-plan-html and https://www.gov.uk/government/news/government-unveils-biggest-overhaul-to-water-in-a-generation
pageInfo.p8: Security and resilience claims draw on the National Security Strategy and National Risk Register. Evidence: https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/national-security-strategy-2025-security-for-the-british-people-in-a-dangerous-world and https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/national-risk-register-2026
pageInfo.p9: Burnham’s No.10 North and devolution proposals are read alongside the English Devolution and Community Empowerment Act. Evidence: https://www.andyburnham.org.uk/speech-29-june and https://www.gov.uk/government/news/english-devolution-bill-receives-royal-assent
pageInfo.p10: The delivery page uses current planning, performance and public-value frameworks. Evidence: https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/planning-and-performance-framework, https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/the-office-for-value-for-money-report and https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/spending-review-2025-document/spending-review-2025-html
pageInfo.p11: Illustrative joint projects derived from the stated strategy and current policy architecture; they are not announced Burnham-government programmes unless subsequently confirmed.
pageInfo.p12: The 20 departments and delivery bodies reproduce the proof-of-concept collection list exactly, checked against https://www.gov.uk/government/organisations
pageInfo.p13: Cross-cutting competencies are implementation capabilities, not additional hierarchical delivery levels.
pageInfo.p14: Policy-status, evidence and caution notes for this proof-of-concept board.
pageInfo.p15: Illustrative live clones and a compact monitoring and evaluation design.
pageInfo.p16: The complete candidate audit method, concentrations, gaps, overlaps and cautions.

measures.title: Real household disposable income per head
measures.mainText: Track inflation-adjusted disposable income per person, with distribution by income group and place.

measures.title: Household essential-cost burden
measures.mainText: Track the share of household resources absorbed by housing, energy, water, food, childcare and transport.

measures.title: Place growth and productivity gap
measures.mainText: Compare employment, earnings, business investment and productivity across local areas and the national median.

measures.title: Housing security and homelessness
measures.mainText: Combine social and council home delivery, affordability, rough sleeping and temporary accommodation indicators.

measures.title: Youth sustained learning and work destinations
measures.mainText: Track education, training, apprenticeship and sustained employment outcomes, including NEET duration and unequal access.

measures.title: Healthy life and integrated-care pressure
measures.mainText: Track healthy life expectancy gaps, elective waits, avoidable admissions, delayed discharge and care continuity.

measures.title: Community safety, justice timeliness and confidence
measures.mainText: Track serious harm, neighbourhood crime, case duration, victim experience and confidence in policing and justice.

measures.title: Affordable clean essentials and resilience
measures.mainText: Track household energy burden, fuel poverty, clean-power delivery, water reliability, pollution and climate-resilience milestones.

measures.title: National readiness and resilience
measures.mainText: Use an agreed classified and public indicator set for military readiness, civil preparedness, stockpiles and critical infrastructure.

measures.title: Devolution and democratic trust
measures.mainText: Track powers and funding consolidated locally, public participation, perceived influence and trust in institutions.

measures.title: Cross-government delivery and public value
measures.mainText: Track milestone reliability, benefit realisation, outcome-adjusted cost, dependency resolution and corrective action.

evalQuestions.questionText: Whose living standards improve, and who remains exposed to unaffordable essentials?
evalQuestions.mainText: Test distribution by income, disability, family type, tenure, nation and place.

evalQuestions.questionText: Does place-based investment create additional good growth in persistently weaker local economies?
evalQuestions.mainText: Compare plausible counterfactuals, displacement, local procurement and sustained productivity.

evalQuestions.questionText: Which housing interventions most reliably create and sustain secure tenancies?
evalQuestions.mainText: Compare supply, Housing First, standards and support pathways over time.

evalQuestions.questionText: Which young people still fail to reach a credible learning or work route, and why?
evalQuestions.mainText: Use linked journeys and lived experience to detect hidden access and retention barriers.

evalQuestions.questionText: Do integrated prevention arrangements improve health while reducing avoidable acute demand?
evalQuestions.mainText: Test service use, wellbeing, equity, carer experience and costs across neighbourhoods.

evalQuestions.questionText: Do safety, border and justice reforms improve both effectiveness and procedural fairness?
evalQuestions.mainText: Examine harm, timeliness, lawful decision-making and experiences of victims, defendants and communities.

evalQuestions.questionText: Are energy, water, environmental and security transitions resilient and fairly shared?
evalQuestions.mainText: Assess affordability, place impacts, preparedness, workforce transition and exposure to shocks.

evalQuestions.questionText: Does devolved, disciplined one-government delivery improve trust and public value?
evalQuestions.mainText: Trace whether authority, finance, data, accountability and adaptation combine to produce visible results.

docContent.p14:
Board Purpose, Policy Status, Sources and Cautions

Proof of Concept - Not Endorsed.

This board is an illustrative strategy and delivery model prepared from public evidence available on 20 July 2026. It is intended to help a Prime Minister and advisers see causal outcomes, joint delivery projects, departmental contributions and cross-government competencies in one navigable model.

Policy status at the evidence cut-off

Andy Burnham’s 29 June 2026 vision speech and 17 July 2026 Labour leadership speech were public. At the time of the final evidence refresh, an official first prime-ministerial Downing Street speech and confirmed Cabinet appointments had not yet been published on official channels. The thirteen joint projects are therefore an evidence-informed design proposal, not a claim that each programme has been announced or funded.

Primary Burnham evidence: 29 June vision speech and 17 July Labour leadership speech.

Interpretation cautions

This is not an official programme, fiscal event, machinery-of-government decision, Cabinet list or forecast. England-only policy instruments must be adapted to the devolution settlements; reserved and devolved responsibilities need legal and intergovernmental review. Box and link wording expresses hypotheses for testing, not settled causal proof. The Sources registry records every public URL relied on.

This Documentation page is to simply illustrate how Documentation Pages within DoView Boards can contain 'clones' of elements within DoView Boards which, if updated elsewhere in a DoView Board are automatically updated on the Documentation Page.

Illustrative Monitoring and Evaluation Plan

The clone blocks below are live references to canonical board elements. They are followed by an illustrative evaluation rhythm; this is not a complete performance framework.

Selected page and box titles

Selected Measures

Selected Evaluation Questions

Selected causal links

Illustrative rhythm

Use monthly delivery reviews for milestones and dependencies; quarterly outcome reviews for distribution, cost and risk; six-monthly synthesis of evaluation findings; and an annual public account of progress, uncertainty and corrective action. Baselines, definitions, owners, data quality and devolution coverage must be agreed before operational use.
docContent.p16:
Alignment review and mapping cautions

Mapping method

Every Level 1 project was compared with every This–Then box; every This–Then box was then reviewed back against all Level 1 projects. Every Level 2 department was compared with every Level 1 project, followed by the reverse project-to-department review. Each no-level competency was compared with relevant outcome, project and department boxes, followed by a reverse target-side review. Only direct, material and defensible relationships were serialized.

Concentrations and lightly mapped areas

Place-based growth, devolution, housing, skills, prevention, resilience and one-government delivery legitimately attract dense support because they are cross-system domains. Lower counts for specialist departments or narrower boxes should not be interpreted as lower importance. The accompanying mapping-audit report gives exact source and target degree distributions, including zero, one, two, three and four-plus incoming-link statistics.

Overlap and missing-capability cautions

Potential overlaps include No.10 North with One Government reform; place funds with connected transport and industrial strategy; local health-and-employment partnerships with national health-and-care integration; and clean-energy reform with defence-industry resilience. Programme charters would need to settle boundaries, senior responsible owners, budgets, statutory powers and common data. Possible missing delivery capabilities include local-government finance reform, regulatory capacity, major-project consenting, benefits administration, workforce supply, cyber assurance and intergovernmental dispute resolution.

Uncertainty and subject-matter review

Mappings involving devolved competence, national security, border operations, water regulation, public procurement, fiscal scoring, justice capacity and health-data sharing require specialist legal, operational, security and economic review. The board should be updated when the first official prime-ministerial speech, Cabinet appointments, machinery-of-government changes, fiscal decisions and programme announcements are published.

Warning: link counts do not prove ownership, resources, accountability, effort, causal strength or performance. They record candidate direct relationships in this illustrative model only.
docContent.p17:
Using DoView Boards and Disclaimer

See the disclaimer section below.

The DoView Board prompt and the DoView Board built within this DoView Board prototype app are provided free of charge so that anyone can experiment with DoView Boards and explore for themselves how they can answer the 20 key questions that anyone running or overseeing an organization needs to answer. See https://doviewplanning.org/doviewboardsuse.

Walk-Through

To get a walk-through for using DoView Boards, see https://doviewplanning.org/walkthrough.

Accessing Consulting or Training

We can provide consulting or training on integrating the use of DoView Boards into your existing planning, implementation and reporting workflows in business, government, or nonprofit settings. See https://doviewplanning.org/offerings.

Adapting the DoView Board Prototype App to your setting

This DoView Board is being used within the DoView Board prototype app. It is a free of charge app that runs inside any browser window and can be used for piloting, testing, proof-of-concept and for use where confidential information is not being included and where there is a low security risk.

The DoView Board app is an open-source developer project under an Apache-2.0 open-source license. See https://github.com/doviewplanning/official-doview-boards. The wider DoView Planning methodology and DoView® trademarks are governed separately. See https://doviewplanning.org/trademarkuse.

Because it is an open-source project, any developer can extend, harden, adapt, or include features of the DoView Board app in other planning products or systems to suit specific deployment requirements. For example, adding enhanced stability, scalability, security, authentication, data handling, or integration features needed for production use, sensitive content, or higher-security environments. If you are looking to deploy DoView Boards in a live operational setting, you can work with any developer to tailor the app to your needs. You can also get in touch with us for advice and methodological input into the best way for you to adapt and extend your use of DoView Boards in your particular setting. https://doviewplanning.org/contact.

Disclaimer

DoView Boards are provided for planning and illustrative purposes only. The content of any DoView Board does not constitute professional advice of any kind, including but not limited to legal, financial, medical, strategic, or organizational advice. No warranty is given as to the accuracy, completeness, or fitness for purpose of any content. The usual precautions for AI-generated material need to be taken. Dr Paul Duignan, DoViewPlanning.org, The Ideas Web Ltd, DoView Corporation Ltd and any associated parties accept no liability whatsoever for any loss, damage, or adverse outcome arising directly or indirectly from the use of, or reliance on, any DoView Board or its contents. Use entirely at your own risk.

Please note that this DoView Board is in an interactive HTML file and contains active JavaScript so the board can work. Only open boards from sources you trust. Read-only copies disable editing through the board interface but are not security protection.
